subject: Geology
title: The Mantle
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Mantle.

hook.text: The mantle makes up 84% of Earth's volume. It's a vast ocean of hot rock that flows like thick honey.

buildup.text: It sits between the crust and the core, roughly 2,900 kilometers thick, made mostly of silicate minerals.

discovery.text: The upper mantle contains the asthenosphere, a soft zone where tectonic plates float and slowly slide.

twist.text: The mantle isn't liquid. It's solid rock that flows very slowly under immense heat and pressure over time.

climax.text: Mantle convection drives plate tectonics. Rising heat pushes plates apart; sinking rock pulls them down.

punchline.text: The mantle is the engine that moves continents.
